disclosure_items.clinical_fact: A constant dry cough for more than 8 weeks, beginning shortly after a mild viral cold
trigger_question_examples: How long has the cough been going on?
When did this start?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's been more than two months now, ever since a cold. It just never went away — a dry, tickly cough all day.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: A persistent tickle/scratch sensation in the throat that is set off by talking, laughing, or breathing cool air
trigger_question_examples: What sets the cough off?
What does it feel like before you cough?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: There's this tickle in my throat, and just talking or laughing or the cold air sets off a fit — I can't control it.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The coughing fits are violent enough to cause gagging and retching
trigger_question_examples: How bad do the fits get?
What happens during a coughing fit?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: They're violent — I cough until I gag and retch. It's exhausting.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: During a severe coughing fit tonight the patient briefly lost consciousness; witnessed by the spouse; recovered spontaneously
trigger_question_examples: You said you passed out — can you tell me exactly what happened?
Did you actually lose consciousness?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes — I was coughing so hard I blacked out for a few seconds. My wife saw it. I came round on my own but felt groggy and shaky.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Hoarse, croaky voice for a period after each severe coughing fit
trigger_question_examples: Has your voice changed?
Do you get hoarse after coughing?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: My voice goes hoarse and croaky for a while after a bad fit, then it comes back.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The cough has not responded to previously tried treatments — an inhaler, reflux tablets, and a nasal spray
trigger_question_examples: Have you tried anything for it?
What treatments have you had so far?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: The doctor tried a puffer, some reflux tablets and a nasal spray over the last couple of months, but none of it made any difference.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Currently uses a dry-powder preventer inhaler (fluticasone); no ACE-inhibitor blood-pressure medication
trigger_question_examples: What medications are you taking now?
Are you on any blood pressure tablets?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Just the dry-powder preventer puffer at the moment. No blood pressure tablets.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Between coughing fits, breathing feels normal with no chest tightness, wheeze, or breathlessness at rest
trigger_question_examples: How is your breathing between the fits?
Do you feel short of breath or wheezy when you're not coughing?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Between the fits my breathing is fine — no wheeze, no tightness. It's only during a fit that I can't catch my breath.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No coughing up blood, no blue lips, no chest pain, and no ongoing loss of consciousness since the single brief episode
trigger_question_examples: Have you coughed up any blood?
Any chest pain, or did your lips go blue?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No blood, no chest pain, my lips didn't go blue, and I've only blacked out that one time — I'm awake and talking now.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Normally fully independent; tonight exhausted and fearful that speaking will trigger another fit and another faint
trigger_question_examples: How are you managing at home normally?
How has this affected you day to day?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I'm usually completely independent, but tonight I'm exhausted and frightened to even talk in case I set off another fit and faint again.
